content.text: Complete this instrumented research flow. Call exactly one tool per turn and wait for each tool result before choosing the next tool. Step 1: call lookup with query "flue instrumentation". Step 2: use the lookup result id as lookupId and call web_search with query "Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation". Step 3: use the first web_search result url and call summarize_source. After summarize_source returns, reply with exactly PROMPT_DONE and no other text.

input.content:
You are running in headless mode with no human operator. Work autonomously — never ask questions, never wait for user input. Make your best judgment and proceed independently.

You are a deterministic Flue instrumentation test agent. Follow user instructions exactly. When asked for a marker, output only that marker and no extra text. When running a local skill file, read it yourself and do not delegate it to a task.

## Available Skills

The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks. When a task matches a skill description, call the `activate_skill` tool with that skill name before proceeding so its full instructions are loaded. Skill instructions and supporting resources stay lazy until activation or explicit file reads.

- **e2e-flue-skill** — Deterministic skill used by the Braintrust Flue instrumentation e2e test.

Date: Tue, Jun 23, 2026
Working directory: /Users/lucaforstner/conductor/workspaces/braintrust-sdk-javascript/belgrade-v1/e2e/.bt-tmp/run-7BJkP3/scenarios/flue-v1-0-0-beta-3

Directory structure:
.agents
.flue
.flue-build
.flue-vite
assertions.ts
cassette-filter.mjs
constants.mjs
node_modules
package.json
pnpm-lock.yaml
scenario.cli.mjs
scenario.impl.mjs
scenario.mjs
scenario.test.ts
scenario.ts

content.text: Reply with exactly TASK_DONE and no other text.

tools.description: Read a file or list a directory. For files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB — use offset/limit for large files. For directories, returns the list of entries.
tools.name: read

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

path.description: Path to the file to read

tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file and parent directories if they do not exist.
tools.name: write

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write

tools.description: Edit a file using exact text replacement. The oldText must match a unique region of the file. Use replaceAll to replace all occurrences.
tools.name: edit

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique)

path.description: Path to the file to edit

tools.description: Execute a bash command. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to the last 2000 lines or 50KB.
tools.name: bash

tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers.
tools.name: grep

literal.description: Match the pattern as literal text

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: .)

tools.description: Find files by filename pattern using shell find -name semantics. Returns matching file paths.
tools.name: glob

path.description: Directory to search in (default: .)

tools.description: Delegate a focused task to a detached child agent with its own context. Use this for independent research, file exploration, or parallel work. Pass attachment IDs shown in the conversation to include those images. The task returns only its final answer to this conversation. No subagents are currently defined.
tools.name: task

agent.description: Declared subagent to use for the child agent

attachments.description: Images from this conversation to include in the child agent prompt

id.description: Attachment ID shown in the current conversation

cwd.description: Working directory for the child agent. AGENTS.md and skills are discovered from here.

description.description: Short human-readable label for the delegated work

prompt.description: Focused instructions for the child agent

tools.description: Load the full instructions for one available skill before performing work that matches its description. Supporting resources remain lazy until explicitly read.
tools.name: activate_skill

input.content:
You are a context summarization assistant. Your task is to read a conversation between a user and an AI coding assistant, then produce a structured summary following the exact format specified.

Do NOT continue the conversation. Do NOT respond to any questions in the conversation. ONLY output the structured summary.

content.text:
<conversation>
[User]: Complete this instrumented research flow. Call exactly one tool per turn and wait for each tool result before choosing the next tool. Step 1: call lookup with query "flue instrumentation". Step 2: use the lookup result id as lookupId and call web_search with query "Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation". Step 3: use the first web_search result url and call summarize_source. After summarize_source returns, reply with exactly PROMPT_DONE and no other text.

[Assistant thinking]: The user wants me to complete an instrumented research flow with specific steps:
1. Call lookup with query "flue instrumentation"
2. Use the lookup result id to call web_search with query "Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation"
3. Use the first web_search result url to call summarize_source
4. After that, reply with exactly "PROMPT_DONE" and no other text

I need to call exactly one tool per turn and wait for each result. Let me start with step 1.

[Assistant]: I'll complete this instrumented research flow step by step. Starting with step 1:

[Assistant tool calls]: lookup(query="flue instrumentation")

[Tool result]: "{\"id\":\"flue-session-2026\",\"query\":\"flue instrumentation\",\"topic\":\"session instrumentation\"}"

[Assistant thinking]: Good, I got the lookup result with id "flue-session-2026". Now I need to proceed to step 2 and call web_search using this id as lookupId and the query "Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation".

[Assistant]: Now proceeding to step 2:

[Assistant tool calls]: web_search(lookupId="flue-session-2026", query="Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation")

[Tool result]: "{\"lookupId\":\"flue-session-2026\",\"query\":\"Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation\",\"results\":[{\"title\":\"Flue reasoning stream instrumentation\",\"url\":\"https://example.test/flue/reasoning-streams\"}]}"

[Assistant thinking]: Perfect! I got the web_search results. There is one result with the URL "https://example.test/flue/reasoning-streams". Now I need to proceed to step 3 and call summarize_source with this URL.

[Assistant]: Now proceeding to step 3:

[Assistant tool calls]: summarize_source(url="https://example.test/flue/reasoning-streams")

[Tool result]: "{\"summary\":\"Flue emits reasoning, tool execution, and LLM turn events separately.\",\"url\":\"https://example.test/flue/reasoning-streams\"}"
</conversation>

This is the PREFIX of a turn that was too large to keep. The SUFFIX (recent work) is retained.

Summarize the prefix to provide context for the retained suffix:

## Original Request
[What did the user ask for in this turn?]

## Early Progress
- [Key decisions and work done in the prefix]

## Context for Suffix
- [Information needed to understand the retained recent work]

Be concise. Focus on what's needed to understand the kept suffix.
